item.title: Google looks to tackle longstanding RCS spam in India — but not alone
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: Google is integrating carrier-level filtering into RCS in India through a partnership with Airtel to strengthen protections against spam.

item.title: OpenAI reveals more details about its agreement with the Pentagon
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: By CEO Sam Altman’s own admission, OpenAI’s deal with the Department of Defense was “definitely rushed,” and “the optics don’t look good.”

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude rises to No. 1 in the App Store following Pentagon dispute
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Anthropic’s chatbot Claude seems to have benefited from the attention around the company’s fraught negotiations with the Pentagon.

item.title: SaaS in, SaaS out: Here’s what’s driving the SaaSpocalypse
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: What's behind the SaaSpocalypse? It simply seems a new supreme has risen.

item.title: The trap Anthropic built for itself
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: Anthropic, OpenAI, Google DeepMind and others have long promised to govern themselves responsibly. Now, in the absence of rules, there's not a lot to protect them.

item.title: The billion-dollar infrastructure deals powering the AI boom
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: Here's everything we know about the biggest AI infrastructure projects, including major spending from Meta, Oracle, Microsoft, Google, and OpenAI.

item.title: OpenAI’s Sam Altman announces Pentagon deal with ‘technical safeguards’
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: OpenAI's CEO claims its new defense contract includes protections addressing the same issues that became a flashpoint for Anthropic.

item.title: Pentagon moves to designate Anthropic as a supply-chain risk
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: "We don't need it, we don't want it, and will not do business with them again," the president wrote in the post.

item.title: Musk bashes OpenAI in deposition, saying ‘nobody committed suicide because of Grok’
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: In his lawsuit against OpenAI, Musk touted xAI safety compared with ChatGPT. A few months later, xAI's Grok flooded X with nonconsensual nude images.

item.title: Anthropic vs. the Pentagon: What’s actually at stake?
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Anthropic and the Pentagon are clashing over AI use in autonomous weapons and surveillance, raising high-stakes questions about national security, corporate control, and who sets the rules for military AI.

item.title: ChatGPT reaches 900M weekly active users
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: OpenAI shared the new numbers as part of its announcement that it has raised $110 billion in private funding.

item.title: Who’s really running AI? Inside the billion-dollar battle over regulation with Alex Bores
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan, Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: The Pentagon is playing chicken with Anthropic over who gets to control how the military uses AI while communities across the country are blocking data center construction. As the AI debate has been flattened to “doomers versus boomers,” one state legislator is attempting to walk a middle road.  On this episode of TechCrunch’s Equity podcast, Rebecca Bellan sits down with Alex Bores, a New York State Assemblymember and candidate for U.S. Congress. Bores sponsored New York’s […]

item.title: AI music generator Suno hits 2M paid subscribers and $300M in annual recurring revenue
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: Suno lets users create music using natural language prompts, making it possible for people with little experience to generate audio with little effort.

item.title: Perplexity’s new Computer is another bet that users need many AI models
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Perplexity Computer, in the company’s words, "unifies every current AI capability into a single system."

item.title: Employees at Google and OpenAI support Anthropic’s Pentagon stand in open letter
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: While Anthropic has an existing partnership with the Pentagon, the AI company has remained firm that its technology not be used for mass domestic surveillance or fully autonomous weaponry.

item.title: Last 24 hours to get TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 tickets at the lowest rates of the year
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: The lowest rates of the year for TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 end after today. Prices go up at 11:59 p.m. PT. Don't miss connecting with 10,000 founders, investors, and operators, and key takeaways from 250+ industry leaders. Register now to save up to $680, or up to 30% on group passes.

item.title: OpenAI raises $110B in one of the largest private funding rounds in history
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: The new funding consists of a $50 billion investment from Amazon as well as $30 billion each from Nvidia and SoftBank, against a $730 billion valuation.

item.title: Jack Dorsey just halved the size of Block’s employee base — and he says your company is next
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: Jack Dorsey has long been an open admirer of Elon Musk. Now, it seems, he may have been taking notes.

item.title: Anthropic CEO stands firm as Pentagon deadline looms
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei said Thursday that he "cannot in good conscience accede" to the Pentagon's demands to give the military unrestricted access to its AI systems.

item.title: So, we’re getting Prada Meta AI glasses, right?
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Mark Zuckerberg was at Prada's fashion week event in Milan, leaving everyone to wonder if we're getting Meta AI glasses under the Prada brand.
